lines.en_lit: And if you wish, say If we hold encircling by foot, everyone agrees that we learn inside outside. And here they disagree about this One Sage, holds encircling by hand; and one Sage, holds encircling by foot.
And if you wish, say Everyone the encircling by hand, and here they disagree about this One Sage, holds we derive hand from foot, And one Sage, holds that we do not derive

And does Rabbi Yosei HaGelili hold the encircling by hand? But from that it is taught the latter clause Rabbi Eliezer says: He stood in place and sprinkled by inference the first tanna, does not maintain Rather, it is clear as we originally answered, one Sage, holds encircling by hand; and one Sage, holds encircling by foot.

And if you wish, say they disagree about this One Sage, holds perimeter of inner altar is like the perimeter of outer altar, and one Sage, holds entire inner altar stands in place of one corner of outer altar.

It was taught Rabbi Yishmael said: Two High Priests remained from the First Temple. This one says: I encircled by hand And that one says: I encircled by foot. This one gave a reason for his statement, and that one gave a reason for his statement.

The one gave reason for his statement: The perimeter of the inner altar like the perimeter of the outer altar, And the one gave reason for his statement: entire inner altar stands in place of one corner of outer

Rabbi Eliezer says: He stood in place and sprinkled whose the mishna? is Rabbi Yehuda, As it was taught Rabbi Meir says Rabbi Eliezer says He stood in place and sprinkled, and on all he presented from above downward, except for that on the diagonal [alakhson] he sprinkled from below upward.

Rabbi Yehuda says Rabbi Eliezer says: He stood in place and sprinkled, and on all of he sprinkled from below upward, except for that one which was directly before him, on which he would present from above downward. so as not to dirty his garments
He sprinkled on pure [tohoro] of the altar. What tohoro? Rabba bar Rav Sheila said: half altar, as people say: Tehar tihara, and it is the middle of the day.

raises an objection: When he sprinkles he sprinkles neither on top of the ash nor on top of the coals; rather, he rakes the coals to both sides and sprinkles. Rather, Rabba bar Rav Sheila on the exposed of altar, as it is written: “And the like of the very heaven for clearness [letohar]”

It was taught Ḥananya says: presents on the north side Rabbi Yosei says: He presents on the south side. With regard to what they disagree? One Sage, holds the entrance positioned in the south, And one Sage, holds the entrance positioned in the north,

Everyone in any case where he finishes the presentations corners, that where he places on top. What is the reason the verse states: and cleanse it and sanctify it” place he sanctified there he shall cleanse
And he would pour the remainder of the blood on the western base of the outer altar. that the verse states “And he shall pour out all the blood of the bull and when he goes out he first reaches that
And he would pour on the outer altar, on its southern base. The Sages taught “The base of the altar” is the southern base. you say the southern base? Or perhaps that is not rather the western base?

You said: Let his descent from the ramp be derived from his exit from the Sanctuary Just as his exit from the Sanctuary on closest to him, and which is that, it is the western base; so too, his descent from the ramp on closest to him, and which is that? It is the southern base,

It was taught Rabbi Yishmael says: this and that, the western base Rabbi Shimon ben Yoḥai says: this and that the southern base. Granted, Rabbi Yishmael maintains not clarified is derived from explicit:
However, Rabbi Shimon ben Yoḥai, what is the reason Rav Ashi said: maintains entrance is positioned at the south
The school of Rabbi Yishmael taught in the school of Rabbi Shimon ben Yoḥai: this and that, the southern base. And your mnemonic The men pulled the man,
These and those mixed in the canal and flow out The Sages taught: misuses by blood, the statement of Rabbi Meir and Rabbi Shimon. And the Rabbis say: not misuse by these
